description_long: This oven is designed to resemble a traditional brick fireplace, featuring a rectangular structure with a prominent brick pattern in varying shades of red, orange, and brown. The front face has a large rectangular opening, simulating a firebox, with two crossed metal rods and colored knobs, possibly representing fire tools or decorative elements. The oven sits on a wide, flat base that extends outward, providing stability and a small ledge. The sides and top are smoothly curved, maintaining the consistent brick motif throughout. The back is flat and unadorned, with the brick pattern continuing seamlessly. There are no visible control panels, handles, or windows, giving it a simple, classic appearance.
description: A brick-patterned oven styled like a traditional fireplace, with a large open front and decorative fire tools. The structure is robust, rectangular, and features a consistent red-orange brick motif.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large open firebox, crossed metal rods with colored knobs, and the extended brick-patterned base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the oven, the curved top, and the protruding fire tools.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a flat, uninterrupted brick-patterned surface with no openings or features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the oven's depth and the side profile of the fire tools.
attribute_control_panel_design: No control panel is visible on any side of the oven. The front is dominated by the firebox opening and decorative rods, with no buttons, knobs, or display elements present.
attribute_door_handle_type: No traditional door handle is visible. The only protruding elements are two crossed metal rods with colored knobs, which may serve a decorative or functional purpose but do not resemble standard handles.
